meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your apps. Learn to implement high-conversion user onboarding to finish your projects. Intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment usually stems from a lack of user momentum. By implementing a structured, value-driven onboarding flow, you force yourself to define the 'Aha!' moment. This creates a clear development roadmap, keeping you focused on shipping features that actually drive user retention rather than getting lost in endless refactoring.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a folder full of half-finished repositories, the excitement of the initial commit fading into the crushing weight of scope creep. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you're failing because you're building features in a vacuum without a clear destination for your user.

Great engineering is about orchestrating a narrative. When you design the onboarding process first, you are forced to map out the exact path a user takes to derive value. This shifts your mindset from 'writing code' to 'delivering outcomes,' providing the structural anchor you need to push your projects across the finish line.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single action that delivers immediate value. If a user doesn't reach this in under 60 seconds, your project is already failing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project intent and define the specific 'Aha!' moment for a first-time user. Create a 3-step onboarding sequence that leads the user directly to this value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Draft user journey map for the onboarding sequence based on the core value proposition.'

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Create a database structure that tracks onboarding progress so you can resume user state and avoid data loss during development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for tracking user onboarding state, including current_step, completed_at, and drop_off_metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file schema.prisma --instruction 'Implement user onboarding progress tracking fields.'

steps.title: Implement Progressive Profiling
steps.description: Don't overwhelm users with forms. Ask for info only when it's contextually necessary for the next step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a UI component flow for progressive onboarding that collects user data incrementally across three distinct steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create React components for a multi-step onboarding wizard with progress state management.'

steps.title: Automate Onboarding Email Triggers
steps.description: If a user stalls, bring them back. Connect your onboarding flow to a simple transactional email service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a backend service module that triggers an email if a user is stuck on step 2 of the onboarding for more than 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file services/email.ts --instruction 'Implement logic to detect onboarding inactivity and trigger follow-up emails.'

steps.title: Validate with Telemetry
steps.description: Add tracking to your onboarding steps so you can see exactly where users drop off, allowing you to optimize your funnel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate analytics event tracking into each step of the onboarding flow to identify user drop-off points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Inject analytics logging into all onboarding navigation functions.'

pro_tips: Treat onboarding as the most important feature; if you can't onboard a user, the rest of your code is effectively invisible.
Use 'feature flagging' to hide the rest of your app until the user completes the onboarding, keeping your development focus narrow.
Always include a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through a mandatory tour is the fastest way to increase bounce rates.
Build your onboarding with mock data first. If the flow doesn't feel rewarding with dummy data, it won't work with real users.
Track the time between 'Sign Up' and 'Aha! Moment' as your primary KPI. Optimize to reduce this delta by 10% every week.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and use a tool like Hotjar to watch 5 real users navigate the process. Refine based on where they click and where they get confused.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop building aimlessly and start shipping products that users actually understand. You have the tools, the strategy, and the expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
